data.title: BOE Could Keep Rates Unchanged in 2026 But Rate Hike Risk Remains — Market Talk
data.description:
1141 GMT - The Bank of England is expected to keep interest rates on hold at 3.75% in 2026, as Middle East tensions persist and given the high uncertainty around the effects of the conflict, Pimco's Peder Beck‑Friis and Konstantin Veit say in a note. The U.K. labor market is weak, which should limit second-round inflationary pressures from high energy prices, they say. "With policy already restrictive, the BOE can likely afford to stay on hold." If energy prices rise further, however, the BOE could raise interest rates to prevent inflation from rising sharply, they say. Investors price in an 84% chance of the BOE keeping interest rates on hold at Thursday's rate decision, LSEG data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

1130 GMT - The euro has scope to rise back above $1.20 if the European Central Bank raises interest rates later this year while the Federal Reserve cuts rates, strategists at UniCredit's The Investment Institute say. The ECB is likely to leave rates unchanged Thursday as it awaits more information on the inflationary implications of the war, they say in a note. However, it could raise rates in June and September, they say. In contrast the Fed could cut rates in December. Tighter rate differentials could drive the euro to its year to date peak of $1.2078 although "not before the current war-driven risk premium has fully unwound." The euro rises 0.2% to $1.1739. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1116 GMT - U.S. Treasury yields edge higher while the dollar falls, with little progress but much hope for a resolution in the Middle East. "Despite the mixed signals, the broader macro impact remains skewed toward higher inflation risks as oil prices extended their rally amid concerns over sustained supply disruptions," says BankPro CEO Paolo Broccardo in a note. This dynamic has pushed Treasury yields higher across maturities, as markets continue to expect a cautious monetary policy, he says. "The latter could offer support to the U.S. currency." The two-year Treasury yield rises 1.5 basis points to 3.791% and the 10-year yield rises 0.4 basis points to 4.314%, according to Tradeweb. The DXY dollar index falls 0.2% to 98.319. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

1107 GMT - The cost of credit default protection for China's sovereign debt declines as the country's industrial profit advances. Data show China's first-quarter industrial profit rose 15.5% year-on-year. "While some areas showed strong profitability due to policy support and export resilience, the recovery appears selective, raising concerns about its sustainability outside a few industries," Tickmill Group's Patrick Munnelly says in a note. China's 5-year credit default swap spreads fall 1 basis point to 44bps in Monday trading, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

1105 GMT - Germany's GfK consumer-confidence barometer follows the recent Ifo and PMI business surveys lower, amid the surge in energy prices from the Iran war, Pantheon Macroeconomics' Claus Vistesen says in a note. The headline index fell to minus 33.3 in May, from minus 28.1, the lowest since January 2023 when confidence was recovering from the hit following Russia's invasion of Ukraine. The data signals a near-term retrenchment in consumer spending, Vistesen says. "We expect consumer spending to fall by 0.2% quarter-to-quarter in the second quarter, after no growth in the first quarter, with growth of 0.2% in the third quarter and fourth quarter." Consumption is set to rise just 0.5% this year, slowing sharply from a 1.7% increase in 2025, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

1059 GMT - Investors appear to be taking profits in bitcoin and ether following any bouts of appreciation this month, IG analyst Chris Beauchamp says in a note. Given the weak performance of bitcoin and ether in recent months, this sell into strength move seems understandable, he says. However, a "weekly close back above the March/April 2025 lows could well see this reverse in a signal that momentum has shifted back in favor of the buyers." This week is busy with central bank decisions, corporate earnings and data but the longer term outlook for crypto is more dependent on developments in the Middle East conflict, he says. Bitcoin falls 0.4% to $77.966, LSEG data show. Ether drops 1.7% to $2,324. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1036 GMT - The Japanese yen is likely to weaken if the Bank of Japan leaves interest rates steady Tuesday and sounds cautious about raising rates in future, Commerzbank's Volkmar Baur says in a note. The BOJ is expected to provide a fairly clear indication that it could raise rates in June, at least if the Iran conflict subsides by then, he says. If such a signal fails to materialize, the warnings from Japan's Ministry of Finance about interventions to prop up the yen will "no longer be of much help." In this case, the dollar could rise above 160 yen, he says. The dollar falls 0.2% to 159.09 yen. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1030 GMT - Potential growth in the eurozone looks set to slow due to restrained productivity, ING's Ruben Dewitte and Peter Vanden Houte say in a note. "Unless the eurozone experiences a sudden acceleration in productivity growth, potential GDP growth is likely to fall below 1%," the analysts say. Productivity has been a significant driver of U.S. growth, while remaining weak in Europe, they say. Italy and Germany even had negative productivity growth in 2024-25. Expanding the labor force could be a key driver, as seen with higher immigration to Spain. However, this is unlikely to be a template for the rest of Europe amid political resistance, they note. Ambitious structural policies will be needed to kick-start productivity, but progress is expected to be slow. (don.forbes@wsj.com)

1010 GMT - Markets will find it harder to ignore the energy crisis as the Middle East conflict drags on and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, Capital Economics says in a note. Chief markets economist Jonas Goltermann says investors widely assume supply disruptions will cause only limited economic damage, a view supporting near-term optimism. But sentiment could fade if conditions on the ground fail to improve. "Every day that passes increases the disruption to energy markets and thereby the downstream damage to economies across the world," he adds. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

1009 GMT - Market optimism over a near-term resolution of the Iran war might soon start to fade if the U.S.-Iran stalemate continues and the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed, says Jonas Goltermann from Capital Economics. "Negotiations to end the U.S.-Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz appear to be floundering yet again, resulting in another cautious start to the week across financial markets," the analyst says. "Time is not on the optimists' side: every day that passes increases the disruption to energy markets and thereby the downstream damage to economies across the world." In afternoon European trading, Brent crude rises 2.4% to $107.88 a barrel, while WTI is up 2.2% to $96.47 a barrel. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0957 GMT - Pimco expects the European Central Bank to keep rates on hold at its April meeting, maintaining a stance of vigilance amid exceptionally high uncertainty, says portfolio manager Konstantin Veit in a note. "At this stage, we continue to expect vigilance rather than action," he says ahead of an ECB policy decision on Thursday. If the ECB were to respond to inflation risks, Pimco expects any move would be measured rather than aggressive. More than two rate hikes would be unlikely, Veit says. With risks to both growth and inflation elevated, policymakers will likely wait for the next round of staff projections in June before adjusting policy, Veit says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0950 GMT - Candriam maintains a neutral stance on U.S. duration and refraining from taking a strong strategic--or long-term--view for now. "Uncertainty around the conflict, the path of oil prices, and the Federal Reserve's reaction function still argue against taking a strong strategic view on nominal Treasuries," the global head of fixed income Nicolas Jullien says in a note. A rapid resolution of the Middle East conflict will undoubtedly lead to a strong relief rally in Treasurys. However, this remains at the mercy of the actions of the U.S. administration, whose next steps are "extraordinarily difficult to forecast," he says. "Sudden reversals from re-escalations remain entirely possible." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Novartis Expected to Take Earnings Hit From Patent Expirations — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Novartis is scheduled to report results for the first quarter on Tuesday. Here is what you need to know:

SALES FORECAST: Analysts expect the Swiss drugmaker to report first-quarter net sales of $13.34 billion, according to consensus estimates compiled by Visible Alpha. For the first quarter of 2025, the company reported net sales of $13.23 billion.

CORE OPERATING PROFIT FORECAST: Novartis is expected to report a quarterly core operating profit--one of its preferred metrics, which strips out exceptional items--of $5.1 billion, according to the same consensus. The company reported a core operating profit of $5.575 billion for last year's first quarter.

Shares in Novartis climbed 10% during the first quarter, but have given up some of the gains in April. The stock rose 3.7% since the start of the year through Friday's close.

WHAT TO WATCH

  • GUIDANCE: Novartis has flagged with its guidance that it expects weakness in the first half of the year, analysts at UBS wrote in a note to clients. The company is facing competition from generic drugs for its heart drug Entresto, leukemia treatment Tasigna, and blood-platelet booster Promacta. It has signaled both net sales and core earnings are expected to fall in the first half. For the year as a whole, Novartis expects net sales growth in the low single digit percentage range at constant currency, and a decline in core operating profit of low single digits. The company's guidance should remain intact after the first-quarter results, analysts at J.P. Morgan said.
  • PIPELINE: Investors will want to know what updates Novartis has in the pipeline for later this year, UBS analysts said. UBS cited drug candidates such as remibrutinib in multiple sclerosis, pelacarsen for cardiovascular risk reduction and del-desiran in myotonic dystrophy type 1, a muscle condition. In addition to the drug pipeline, any updates on the trajectory of recently launched drugs or those that have been approved for new indications might be in focus, analysts at Bank of America said, citing Kisqali for breast cancer, Scemblix for leukemia, Pluvicto for prostate cancer and Rhapsido for hives.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Domino's Continues to Steal Market Share from Competitors — Market Talk
data.description: Domino's Pizza is scooping up market share, CEO Russell Weiner says. The pizza chain on logs positive order count and same-store sales growth in the U.S. in F1Q, despite facing what Weiner calls an intensifying macro and competitive environment. The quarterly results come as pizza chains are engaged in price wars with each another and other fast food vendors, as inflation-weary consumers pull back on eating out. Meanwhile, sales growth at pizza restaurants has lagged behind the broader fast-food market for years. Domino's, though, is bucking some of the broader malaise, Weiner says. "My belief that we can continue to outperform our competition and take meaningful share in 2026 and beyond remains as strong as it has ever been," he says in an earnings release. Domino's is off 5% premarket. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Weight Watchers to Use Up to $40M to Prepay Term Loan, Reaffirms 2026 Guidance
data.description:
Weight Watchers intends to use up to $40 million in cash to prepay and reduce the principal of its outstanding term loan.

Key Highlights:

  • $25M–$30M cash prepayment of the annual cash sweep due June 24, 2026 to reduce term loan principal.
  • Up to $10M voluntary solicitation to prepay term loan at a discount, expected Apr 27–Apr 30, 2026.
  • Company reaffirmed Q1 2026 end-of-period subscriber estimates and full-year 2026 financial guidance.
  • Management says move strengthens liquidity and deleverages balance sheet while supporting continued investments.

data.title: Miluna Acquisition to Combine With Kukugan Invest in $250 Million All-Stock Deal With Earnout
data.description:
Miluna Acquisition signed a Business Combination Agreement with Kukugan Invest and CADV Ventures to complete its initial business combination. The all-stock deal values the transaction at $250 million (Class A shares at $10) and includes up to 5 million earnout shares tied to 2027 revenue. Miluna also entered into sponsor and parent support agreements to secure key votes and consents, and agreed to execute registration rights and lock-up agreements at closing to facilitate post-merger trading. The transaction, unanimously approved by relevant boards, is expected to close in the second half of 2026, subject to shareholder approvals and customary conditions.

Agreement 1: Miluna Acquisition to Combine With Kukugan Invest in $250 Million All-Stock Deal With Earnout

  • Agreement type: Business Combination Agreement (SPAC merger; Parent into Purchaser; Company becomes subsidiary)
  • Counterparty: Kukugan Invest and CADV Ventures
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 23 2026 / Apr 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing or termination (Outside Date: Jan 23 2027)
  • Reason: Complete initial business combination and become public operating company

Agreement 2: Miluna Acquisition Secures Sponsor Support to Back Proposed Business Combination

  • Agreement type: Sponsor Support Agreement
  • Counterparty: MilunaC Technology and Kukugan Invest
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 23 2026 / Apr 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until shareholder vote and closing
  • Reason: Ensure sponsor votes and consents for the merger

Agreement 3: Miluna Acquisition Obtains Parent Shareholder Support for Merger

  • Agreement type: Parent Support Agreement
  • Counterparty: Kukugan Invest shareholders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 23 2026 / Apr 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing
  • Reason: Secure parent shareholder approvals and waivers

Agreement 4: Miluna Acquisition to Enter Registration Rights and Lock-Up to Support Post-Merger Resale

  • Agreement type: Registration Rights Agreement and Lock-Up Agreement (forms to be executed at closing)
  • Counterparty: Sponsor and certain Kukugan Invest shareholders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 23 2026 / Apr 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until registration obligations and lock-up periods expire
  • Reason: Provide resale rights and orderly post-closing trading

Original SEC Filing: Miluna Acquisition Corp [ MMTX ] - 8-K - Apr. 27, 2026

data.title: German Reforms Support Business, Consumer Confidence — Market Talk
data.description: Germany is gearing up for a new wave of structural reform which could boost sentiment, Marion Muehlberger and Ursula Walther at Deutsche Bank say in a note. The government is planning a broad package of reforms, including healthcare, tax and pension changes. The economic impact of reforms will likely be modest in the short term, the analysts say. Still, the changes could provide a much-needed boost to confidence. "New reform momentum might affect corporate and household sentiment positively at a crucial juncture, when the energy price spike has become more than a speed bump on Germany's road to recovery," they say. From 2027, the boost to disposable income could be tangible. Still, while progress looks likely, resistance to reforms remains a headwind, the analysts say. (don.forbes@wsj.com)

data.title: Britain's financial regulator consults on changes to IPO research
data.description:
By Charlie Conchie

Britain's financial regulator has launched a consultation on plans to scrap rules governing the publication of research during initial public offerings (IPOs) in its latest bid to boost the appeal of London as a listing venue.

The Financial Conduct Authority said on Monday it is seeking views on changes that will remove a requirement for a seven‑day delay before research can be published by banks working on an IPO, as well as rules that require firms to provide independent analysts with the same information as their own research analysts.

While the current rules, introduced in 2018, were designed to encourage the publication of independent research on new IPOs, the FCA said they had introduced complexity and risk to the listing process and failed to deliver the intended benefits.

"We are committed to reducing friction, supporting growth, and ensuring the UK remains a competitive and trusted place for companies to raise capital," said Jon Relleen, director of infrastructure & exchanges, supervision, policy & competition division.

The consultation comes after the regulator committed to changing the research rules in a letter to the prime minister in December last year. It will run until May 29.

data.title: China Bans Meta's Acquisition of Manus on National Security Grounds — WSJ
data.description:
By Raffaele Huang

China has ordered that Meta Platforms' $2.5 billion acquisition of artificial-intelligence startup Manus be unwound.

China's National Development and Reform Commission, which has the authority to review foreign investments, said Monday that it has banned the acquisition and ordered it to be rescinded on national security grounds.

"The transaction complied fully with applicable law," a Meta representative said in an email statement. "We anticipate an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."

Manus has developed an AI agent that can carry out sophisticated tasks such as writing in-depth research reports and preparing presentation slides. Early versions of Manus were created by engineers at Beijing Butterfly Effect Technology, which was founded in China in 2022.

Last year, a Singapore-based entity, also called Butterfly Effect, took over the operations of the AI agent product in markets outside China. Manus later relocated most of its China-based employees to Singapore after receiving investment from a California-based venture-capital firm.

Then, in late December, Meta acquired Manus. Days later, Chinese authorities announced a review of the deal in January, saying that cross-border acquisitions and the export of technology must comply with the law.

Last month, Chinese officials called the two co-founders of Manus — Xiao Hong and Ji Yichao — in to discuss the deal in Beijing. The executives, who currently work for Meta, were later told not to leave the country during the investigation, The Wall Street Journal has reported. Many of Manus's top executives are Chinese nationals.

According to Chinese law, any foreign investments that may carry a national-security risk may be subject to review by the authorities. Chinese authorities believe they have the power to demand that the deal be unwound because Beijing Butterfly Effect Technology remains a Chinese company.

After the acquisition, Meta said that there would be no continuing Chinese ownership interest in Manus and that the startup would discontinue its China-based services and operations. Manus was working to close Beijing Butterfly Effect Technology, but hasn't yet done so.

The series of events that culminated in Meta's purchase of China-originated technology has angered Chinese regulators. They are worried that the steps Manus took would spur other Chinese companies to follow suit and move out of China without Beijing's approval.

The inquiry into the Manus sale reflected Beijing's broader attempts to protect the country's AI know-how at a time when the U.S. and China are locked in an intensifying technology race. Both countries have tightened export controls, restricted the exchange of tech professionals and curbed cross-border investment.

Manus and Xiao didn't immediately respond to requests for comment. Ji couldn't be reached for comment.

Write to Raffaele Huang at raffaele.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Romania rejects all bids at February 2038 bond tender and sells 363.0 million lei of October 2029 bonds
data.description:
Romania rejected all bids at a tender to sell February 2038 bonds on Monday, central bank data showed.

Romania also sold a less than planned 363.0 million lei worth of October 2029 bonds, with the average accepted yield at 6.77%.

Debt managers, who had planned to sell 300.0 million lei of the February 2038 paper, last tendered the bonds in February at an average yield of 6.66%. They had also planned to sell 400.0 million lei of the October 2029 bonds, and last successfully tendered them in February at an average yield of 6.11%.

data.title: Eli Lilly to Buy Ajax Therapeutics for Up to $2.3 Billion — Update
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

Eli Lilly continued its recent buying spree, agreeing to pay up to $2.3 billion for Ajax Therapeutics in a deal that bolsters the drugmaker's blood-cancer portfolio.

Eli Lilly on Monday said Ajax is developing next-generation Janus kinase, or JAK, inhibitors for patients with myeloproliferative neoplasms, a group of rare, potentially life-threatening blood cancers that occur when the bone marrow makes too many blood cells.

Ajax's lead asset, AJ1-11095, is an investigational, once-daily first-in-class Type II JAK2 inhibitor currently in a Phase 1 study in patients with myelofibrosis who have previously been treated with a Type I JAK2 inhibitor, Eli Lilly said.

JAK inhibitors are therapeutic agents that block Janus kinase enzymes to treat diseases.

Eli Lilly said that while the currently approved Type I JAK2 inhibitors provide clinical and symptomatic relief, many patients often discontinue treatment due to a lack of durable benefit or loss of response.

Ajax's AJ1-11095 was designed as a selective Type II JAK2 inhibitor to deliver deeper and more durable efficacy than existing JAK2 inhibitors and to provide a novel treatment option for patients who become resistant to Type I inhibitors, the Indianapolis company said.

Eli Lilly, which participated in a $95 million funding round for Ajax in 2024, said the $2.3 billion purchase price includes an undisclosed upfront payment and subsequent payments based on the achievement of certain clinical and regulatory milestones.

Eli Lilly's deal to buy Ajax adds to a recent spate of pharma acquisitions between $1 billion and $10 billion, including several by Lilly, that have become a sweet spot for companies seeking to bolster the work of their own labs or lineups.

Eli Lilly, which is flush with cash from sales of its weight-loss drugs, last week said it would buy privately held cancer biotech Kelonia Therapeutics for up to $7 billion in cash, including an upfront payment of $3.25 billion.

The company struck deals in the first quarter to buy sleep drugmaker Centessa Pharmaceuticals for an initial $6.3 billion, genetic-medicine biotechnology company Orna Therapeutics for up to $2.4 billion and biotech Ventyx Biosciences for about $1.2 billion.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: Samsung Elec to withdraw home appliances, TV sales from China this year, Nikkei reports
data.description:
South Korea's Samsung Electronics will withdraw the sale of its home appliances and TV sales business from China within this year, the Nikkei newspaper reported on Monday.

The company aims to make a final decision on discontinuing sales of home appliances and televisions in China as early as the end of April, Nikkei said, citing unnamed sources.

Samsung Electronics will retain its local production in China for home appliances such as refrigerators, washing machines and air conditioners, using the facilities as supply hubs for overseas markets, the report said.

Nikkei said the biggest reason for the withdrawal is declining price competitiveness, adding that Chinese manufacturers, in addition to offering low product prices, have improved quality and are strengthening their presence in global markets.

Samsung Electronics said in a statement that it regularly reviews its global business structure in line with changes in the operating environment, adding that nothing has been decided regarding market speculation about a potential restructuring of its operations in China.

data.title: Classover Named to TIME's America's Top EdTech Companies of 2026
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 27, 2026 / Classover Holdings Inc. ("Classover" or the "Company"), an AI-driven education company, today announced it has been named to the 2026 list of America's Top EdTech Companies by TIME. TIME partnered with data provider Statista to perform a comprehensive analysis of the top 250 education technology companies in the United States based on their financial strength (70%) and industry impact (30%). Statista tracked more than 2,500 companies for the 2026 U.S. study. Classover ranked in the top half of the list, 122nd place out of 250, recognizing Classover among the most influential and financially resilient education companies in the United States and further marking Classover's successful expansion into high-growth AI and robotics.

"Being named by TIME as one of the top edtech companies in America is a testament to our team's dedication to redefining how children learn outside the traditional classroom," said Stephanie Luo, CEO of Classover. "This ranking validates our strategic pivot toward 'embodied AI.' At Classover, we aren't just putting lessons on screens; we are building an integrated AI infrastructure that teachers, students and parents can all benefit from."

The 2026 ranking reflects Classover's expansion into high-margin, scalable technologies that differentiate it from standard online tutoring as follows:

  • Pioneering AI & Companion Robotics: Classover has solidified its position at the intersection of hardware and software through strategic collaborations with industry leaders like ICreate Education Technology, Luka, and Walimaker. These partnerships are bringing AI coding companions and robotics into the classrooms, shifting student engagement from passive screen-time to hands-on, construction-based learning.

  • AI-Driven Productivity: Through its recent introduction of Tutor Studio, Classover is leveraging AI agents to revolutionize course creation. By embedding the proprietary AI Tutor directly into the teaching workflow, Classover can now rapidly deploy and scale courses without a proportional increase in staffing costs-creating a highly efficient, high-margin model for global expansion.

  • Proprietary Data Advantage: Classover's growth is fueled by its substantial dataset of over 450,000 hours of live teaching experience. Unlike passive learning platforms, Classover focuses on small-group, instructor-led sessions that foster social interaction and critical thinking across over 30 subjects, including Coding, Math, Art, and Languages.

"The future of EdTech lies in AI that streamlines the work of teachers while personalizing the journey for students," added Stephanie Luo. "With our AI Robotics division and our agent-powered Tutor Studio, we are not just participating in the market-we are defining the next generation of global education infrastructure."

The full list of America's Top EdTech Companies of 2026 can be found on TIME.com.

About Classover

Classover Holdings Inc. is an AI-driven education technology company transforming extensive live teaching experience into proprietary AI-powered learning systems. By integrating artificial intelligence, AI agents, and robotics, Classover is building the next generation of global education infrastructure designed to make learning outcomes measurable, verifiable, and accessible across borders. Classover believes focused investment in AI, intelligent agents, and robotics aligns with the Company's mission and positions it to capture the next wave of educational technology innovation.

Forward-Looking Statement

This press release contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the safe harbor provisions of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Forward-looking statements are neither historical facts nor assurances of future performance. Instead, they are based only on Classover's current beliefs, expectations and assumptions regarding the future of Classover's business, future plans and strategies, projections, anticipated events and trends, the economy and other future conditions. data.title: Seer Board Rejects Radoff-JEC Group's Sweetened Take-Private Bid
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Seer's board has rejected an upgraded offer by minority shareholders Bradley Radoff and Michael Torok to take the company private.

The biotechnology company on Monday said that the board has unanimously rejected a bid by the activist investors and minority shareholders operating under the Radoff-JEC Group on the grounds that the $2.35-a-share offer undervalued the business.

Last week, Bradley Radoff and Michael Torok, who own about 7.6% of Seer's shares, submitted a nonbinding proposal to buy the company at the new cash price, which includes a plus a contingent value right. The $2.35 per-share offer was an upgrade from $2.25 a share originally submitted on April 13.

Shares of the company are up 7.7% year-to-date and closed on Friday at $1.97 a share.

The Seer board said its board is confident that the company's strategy, platform and team can create value in excess of the proposal from the Radoff-JEC Group.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Big Banks Raise Oil Forecasts — WSJ
data.description:
By Rebecca Feng

Oil price forecasts are shooting higher once again.

Strategists at both Goldman Sachs and Citigroup increased their estimates for crude after President Trump said he wouldn't send U.S. envoys to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran. With no peace deal in sight, traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains paralyzed.

Goldman now projects Brent futures to reach $90 a barrel in the fourth quarter, up from its previous forecast of $80 a barrel. The bank said production in the Persian Gulf would be slower to recover than expected, and that exports from the region wouldn't normalise until the end of June.

The bank's analysts also warned of spillover economic risks citing unusually high prices for refined products, product shortages and the unprecedented scale of the shock. Some 14.5 million barrels of crude production are being lost every day, they wrote in a note to clients.

Citigroup also lifted its price forecast for Brent, up to $110 a barrel for the second quarter from $95 before. The bank's analysts now expect the Strait of Hormuz to reopen at the end of May instead of mid- to late-April. Flows through the strait could easily remain disrupted through the end of June, in which case Brent could average $130 a barrel in the second quarter, they said.

"Critically, even once flows resume, the need to rebuild both commercial and strategic inventories implies structurally stronger prices for years rather than months," the Citi analysts said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Bitcoin Slips Below $78K, Altcoins Lag: Oil Rally Amid Hormuz Tensions Weighs On Risk Assets
data.description:
Key points:
  • Rising tensions near the Strait of Hormuz and developments in Iran talks supported higher oil prices.
  • Analysts continue to see upside potential for Bitcoin, with targets above $80,000.
  • Liquidations totaled around $300 million, with Ethereum bets accounting for a larger share than Bitcoin.

Bitcoin (BTC) edged lower on Monday morning after Iran peace talks and a fresh escalation in the Strait of Hormuz pushed oil prices higher.

Bitcoin’s price edged 0.3% lower in the last 24 hours to around $77,800 after crossing $79,000 in the previous week. Retail sentiment around the apex cryptocurrency on Stocktwits trended in ‘bullish’ territory over the past day, accompanied by chatter at ‘normal’ levels.

Iran has reportedly floated a new proposal to the U.S. to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the conflict, while suggesting nuclear talks be delayed. President Donald Trump on Saturday canceled plans to send envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner for ceasefire talks, saying negotiations could occur by phone, while Iran said no meeting is scheduled.

On Sunday, tensions near the Strait intensified after Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps boarded two container ships. Oil climbed, with WTI and Brent moved 0.5% to around $95 and $100, respectively.

The United States Oil Fund (USO) moved 0.5% in pre-market trade. Retail sentiment around the fund trended in ‘bearish’ territory over the past day, accompanied by chatter at ‘low’ levels.

Analysts Believe Bitcoin Can Cross $80,000 – Eventually

“The markets are still shaping up for more upside, and it's [Bitcoin] still holding crucial levels,” MN Fund founder Michael van de Poppe wrote in a post on X, “I think that we'll see $85-88K in May and correct/consolidate from there.”

Crypto analyst Ted Pillows stated that Bitcoin remains above the key level of $76,000 after being rejected from the $79,000 to $80,000 band. “As long as the $76,000 support zone holds, Bitcoin could grab the liquidity above the $80,000 level,” he wrote in a post on X.

Cardano, Solana Lead Morning Losses

Cardano (ADA) and Solana (SOL) led losses among the top 10 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization. ADA’s price fell 1.9% in the last 24 hours, while SOL’s price fell 1.6%. Retail sentiment around Cardano remained in ‘bullish’ territory over the past day, while sentiment around Solana flipped to ‘bullish’ from the ‘bearish’ zone. Chatter around both altcoins was at ‘high’ levels.

Ethereum (ETH), Binance Coin (BNB), and Dogecoin (DOGE) edged 0.6% lower in the last 24 hours. Ethereum’s price held above $2,300, and Dogecoin’s price remained below $0.10. Ripple’s XRP (XRP) fell 0.8%, holding above $1.40.

The overall cryptocurrency market was 0.3% lower at $2.67 trillion. CoinGlass data showed nearly $300 million was liquidated in the last 24 hours, with a near-even split between short and long positions. Bets on Ethereum (ETH) led the liquidation wave with $110 million, followed by Bitcoin (BTC) at $95 million.

data.title: OceanFirst receives all regulatory approvals for Flushing merger; closing expected by June 1
data.description:
OceanFirst announced receipt of all requisite regulatory and shareholder approvals to complete its merger with Flushing, with closing expected no later than June 1, 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Federal Reserve approved the OceanFirst–Flushing merger on April 24, 2026, completing required federal sign-off.
  • NY State Department of Financial Services and OCC approvals were granted March 23 and April 6, 2026, respectively.
  • Both companies previously received shareholder approval on April 2, 2026; no further regulatory approvals are required.
  • Parties anticipate closing the transaction no later than June 1, 2026, subject to remaining customary closing conditions.
  • OceanFirst will hold its 2026 virtual Annual Meeting on May 27, 2026; record date for voting was April 2, 2026.

Original SEC Filing: OCEANFIRST FINANCIAL CORP [ OCFC ] - 8-K - Apr. 27, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: NexMetals Reports LCTs Showing 81% Cu Recovery, 10.9% Ni Grade and Improved Recoveries at Selkirk
data.description:
NexMetals reported locked-cycle metallurgical results showing 81.3% copper recovery and a 10.9% nickel concentrate grade at Selkirk.

Key Highlights:

  • Initial LCTs confirm production of separate high-grade Cu and Ni concentrates, offering an alternative to historical bulk concentrate.
  • Copper concentrate: 30.2% Cu grade with 81.3% Cu recovery; nickel in Cu conc minimal (0.62%), reducing penalties.
  • Nickel concentrate: 10.9% Ni grade with 54.4% Ni recovery, a 60% grade uplift vs 2024 MRE inputs.
  • Copper recovery up 16% and copper losses to tailings cut by 56% versus 2024 MRE, improving project recoveries and economics.
  • PGEs, gold, silver and cobalt now assayed; New MRE incorporating LCT and resampling slated for completion in Q2 2026.

Original SEC Filing: NexMetals Mining Corp. [ NEXM ] - 8-K - Apr. 27, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Flushing Financial to Merge into OceanFirst after Federal Reserve Approval; Close Expected by June 1
data.description:
Flushing Financial received final regulatory approval for its merger with OceanFirst and expects the transaction to close by June 1, 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Federal Reserve approved the Flushing–OceanFirst merger on April 24, 2026, completing required regulatory consents.
  • NYDFS and OCC approvals were previously granted on March 23 and April 6, 2026, respectively.
  • Both companies’ shareholders had approved the transaction on April 2, 2026.
  • Parties expect to close the merger no later than June 1, 2026, subject to remaining customary closing conditions.
  • Joint press release filed as Exhibit 99.1 and incorporated by reference in the 8-K.

Original SEC Filing: FLUSHING FINANCIAL CORP [ FFIC ] - 8-K - Apr. 27, 2026
